benchmark: Adaptive retrieval — does a system's memory improve held-out retrieval?
scorer: synaptic's own tests/benchmark/metrics.py (reciprocal_rank, k=10) — byte-identical to eval/metrics.py. Not a metric we wrote.
why_this_axis: It is the deepest difference between the projects: synaptic-*memory* is stateful and learns (Hebbian reinforcement of nodes/edges); OmniFuse's retrieval is otherwise stateless. Neither side had measured whether the dynamics improve retrieval QUALITY — synaptic's own memory eval (eval/scripts/memory_operating_poc.py) is a contract smoke-gate.

mandatory_controls.placebos: shuffled (query<->chunk pairing permuted) and random-q (a random other feedback query). Query-conditional memory must die under both.
mandatory_controls.coverage_split: covered = eval queries whose relevant chunk remembers something; uncovered = those whose relevant chunk remembers nothing. Memory cannot move uncovered queries. If it does, the effect is a corpus-wide scoring artifact.
mandatory_controls.note: Without these controls the benchmark reports a win that is not there. It did for us — see the retraction below.

what_synaptic_reinforcement_actually_rea.finding: In the benchmarked version, SynapticGraph.search() reads NONE of the fields reinforce() writes. ResonanceScorer (the only consumer of node.success_count/access_count) is used by search.py and agent_search.py, not by graph.search; neither EvidenceSearch nor graph_expander reads edge.weight. The only channel is the new edges reinforce() creates on success.
what_synaptic_reinforcement_actually_rea.correction: An earlier revision reported synaptic's Hebbian as HARMFUL (-0.0174). Re-running the same configuration gave -0.0045, so synaptic's warm pass is not deterministic across runs. The fair statement is that Hebbian is NOT WIRED into this version's retrieval and its measured deltas are noise around zero — not that it hurts.

axis_1_disjoint_queries.question: does memory generalize across DIFFERENT questions?
axis_1_disjoint_queries.answer: No — for either system. And an honest mechanism must show exactly that.

omnifuse_feedback_NFCorpus_split1.reading: no signal, and Δuncovered is exactly 0.0000 — the collection's IDF is provably untouched

axis_2_requery_paraphrase.question: does memory serve the same need asked in different words? (this is what memory is for)
axis_2_requery_paraphrase.setup: 5,234 KRA chunks (dev-xgen). 215 questions, each answered by one gold chunk. Feedback = the ORIGINAL question remembered on its gold chunk, for the odd-indexed half only. Eval = LLM paraphrases of all 215 questions (token Jaccard 0.43). The even-indexed half is therefore a built-in uncovered control.
axis_2_requery_paraphrase.reading: real beats the strongest placebo by 5.2x on KRA and is the only positive variant on NFCorpus, so the (query, chunk) pairing carries the signal. synaptic moves nothing on either corpus, because its reinforcement is not wired into its retrieval.
axis_2_requery_paraphrase.generalization: Replicated on a second corpus, a second language and a different relevance structure. On NFCorpus BOTH placebos go NEGATIVE while real stays positive, and Δuncovered is exactly 0.0000 — the cleanest possible signature. Effect size is much smaller there (+0.0460 vs +0.4167 covered) because the cold score is already 0.556: with 38 relevant documents per query, hitting one is easy and there is little headroom. Memory pays most where relevance is concentrated (one right chunk) and the cold ranking is weak.
axis_2_requery_paraphrase.note_2026_07_10: Re-measured after the Korean copula-interrogative stemmer fix changed the cold ranking. real still beats the strongest placebo by ~18x on KRA covered queries, and NFCorpus Δuncovered remains exactly 0.0000.

the_mechanism.idea: A confirmed query becomes evidence about the chunk. It is indexed as a BM25F EVIDENCE field: its terms score the chunk but never enter document frequency, and it is not length-normalized.
the_mechanism.why_df_exclusion: Our retracted design injected the remembered query into the chunk BODY. That raised the document frequency of query vocabulary and deflated its IDF corpus-wide — an accidental, uncontrolled idf_pow reduction. Excluding evidence from df makes the cold store bit-identical and makes Δuncovered structurally zero.
the_mechanism.why_no_length_normalization: Remembering a second query must not dilute the first. With BM25F's collection-average normalization, a memory field held by 2% of chunks explodes fnorm and annihilates the signal (measured: covered gain collapses from +0.4167 to +0.0742).
the_mechanism.evidence_only_terms: A term seen only in evidence has content-df 0, so it takes its IDF from the evidence df. Otherwise the words that memory exists to contribute — the ones absent from the chunk — would be discarded.
the_mechanism.no_tuning: The evidence field carries the same weight as the body (1.0). An empty evidence field contributes nothing, so a cold store is bit-identical — verified on finreg and the full public suite.

retraction_history.what_we_claimed: OmniFuse won this axis by remembering queries and indexing them in the chunk body (NFCorpus +0.0019/+0.0076, MIRACL-ko +0.0618/+0.0729).
retraction_history.how_it_died: Permuting the pairing kept the gain (+0.0555 on MIRACL-ko); attaching a RANDOM feedback query scored better than the real one (+0.0665); and held-out queries whose relevant chunks remembered nothing still gained +0.0441. A control memory of tokens that can never match a query moved nothing (+0.0000 exactly), pinning the effect on term statistics rather than the pairing.

honest_limitations: synaptic was driven through graph.search + graph.reinforce (FTS, no embedder). Its consolidation cascade IS now measured (see synaptic_consolidation_measured_2026_07_10: it runs, promotes 54 nodes, and moves retrieval by exactly 0.0000). Its embedder and reranker remain unmeasured — the shared-embedder full-pipeline track covers the embedder question separately. Its resonance-scored paths (search.py HybridSearch, agent_search.py) are not what graph.search calls in this version.
The re-query axis uses LLM-generated paraphrases of LLM-generated questions over a private corpus; the corpus is not committed. Absolute MRR is a lower bound, and the comparison is symmetric.

Memory is incremental and reversible as of 2026-07-10: remember() folds a confirmed pair into the live index in ~1 ms and forget() withdraws one at the same cost, both bit-identically to a rebuild (eval/results/incremental_memory.json).
Re-query axis replicated on two corpora (ko/en). The disjoint axis was run on NFCorpus and MIRACL-ko. Broader sweeps remain future work.

static_suite_reverification_after_eviden.claim: adding evidence_fields to BM25F changed no static score
bit_identical: finreg single-hop 0.8400 / nDCG 0.8663 / hit 114 / multi-hop 107 / R@10 0.9250

prior_art.borrowed: Indexing confirmed queries as a document FIELD is standard: BM25F was defined over title/body/anchor-text (Robertson & Zaragoza), and query-click logs as a field are reported as the single most important field in machine-learned BM25F (Svore & Burges). Blending df across fields is what Elasticsearch cross_fields does.
prior_art.ours: The opposite of that blend: evidence is excluded from document frequency entirely and is not length-normalized. Chosen because the placebo/uncovered controls forced it, not for novelty — and it is what makes delta-uncovered structurally zero, and what later made the incremental update local.
prior_art.no_novelty_claim: Two web searches found no prior art for the df-exclusion. That is not evidence of absence. We make no novelty claim.
prior_art.not_borrowed_from_synaptic: synaptic's reinforce() is genuinely incremental (a SQLite write), but in the benchmarked version graph.search() reads none of the fields it writes, so there was nothing to copy.

synaptic_consolidation_measured_2026_07_.question: does synaptic's consolidation cascade (maintain = consolidate + decay + prune) change retrieval?
synaptic_consolidation_measured_2026_07_.setup: KRA re-query axis, synaptic's own graph.search (FTS, embedder=None), scored by its own metrics.reciprocal_rank. Two fresh stores: (a) cold -> maintain(), (b) cold -> reinforce (Hebbian warm) -> maintain().

maintain_on_warm.consolidated: 54 node promotions (fed by reinforce success counts)

synaptic_consolidation_measured_2026_07_.reading: The cascade RUNS — reinforcement feeds it and it promotes 54 nodes, decay touches all 5,234 — but graph.search() reads none of it: retrieval delta is exactly +0.0000 on both stores. Same channel isolation as reinforce(). Being maintained is not the same as being wired into retrieval.
synaptic_consolidation_measured_2026_07_.caveat: TTL expiry (72h/90d/365d) cannot trigger on a fresh ingest; what is measured is promotion + decay + prune on the benchmark timescale, i.e. the parts that could possibly move a same-session benchmark.

remeasured_at_default_1.2_2026_07_10.why: the idf_pow default moved 1.5 -> 1.2 (see idf_pow_ablation.json); the memory axes were re-measured at the shipping default
remeasured_at_default_1.2_2026_07_10.reading: the signature strengthens at 1.2: real beats the strongest placebo 6.8x (KRA) / 40x (NFCorpus) on covered, both placebos die, and real's d_uncovered is exactly 0.0000 on NFCorpus.
